title: Method for checking the correct assembling of a twin-tube hydraulic shock-absorber, particularly for a vehicle suspension
abstract: Method for checking the correct assembling of a twin-tube hydraulic shock-absorber, par- ticularly for a vehicle suspension The method comprises the steps of: a) measuring the Barkhausen noise on the outer cylin- drical tube (12) of the shock-absorber (10) before a final closing step by which the outer cylindrical tube (12) is closed at its top end so as to lock a sealing member (30) in its posi- tion, clamping it with a given axial compression load; b) measuring the Barkhausen noise on the outer cylindrical tube (12) once the final closing step has been carried out; c) calcu- lating the difference (Împ) between the Barkhausen noise measured at step b) and the Barkhausen noise measured at step a); and d) comparing the difference (Împ) calculated at step c) with a given minimum level of difference, which minimum level is selected in ad- vance in such a manner that said difference (Împ) is higher than said minimum level when the shock-absorber (10) is correctly assembled, i.e. when the outer cylindrical tube (12) is correctly closed at its top end, whereas said difference (Împ) is lower than said minimum level when the shock-absorber (10) is not correctly assembled, i.e. when the outer cylindri- cal tube (12) is not correctly closed at its top end.
claims:
Claims 1. Method for checking the correct assembling of a twin-tube hydraulic shock- absorber (10), particularly for a vehicle suspension, wherein the shock-absorber (10) comprises an outer cylindrical tube (12), an inner cylindrical tube (14) arranged coaxially with the outer cylindrical tube (12), a piston (18) slida- bly received in the inner cylindrical tube (12), a rod (24) attached to the piston (18) and projecting from the top end of the outer cylindrical tube (12), and a sealing member (30) arranged to sealingly close the outer cylindrical tube (12) at the top end thereof, and wherein the shock-absorber (10) is assembled by a method comprising a final closing step whereby the outer cylindrical tube (12) is closed at its top end so as to lock the sealing member (30) in its position, clamping it with a given axial compression load, the method comprising the steps of: a) measuring the Barkhausen noise on the outer cylindrical tube (12) before said final closing step; b) measuring the Barkhausen noise on the outer cylindrical tube (12) after said final closing step has been carried out; c) calculating the difference (Amp) between the Barkhausen noise measured at step b) and the Barkhausen noise measured at step a); and d) comparing the difference (Amp) calculated at step c) with a given minimum level, said minimum level being selected in advance in such a manner that said difference (Amp) is higher than said minimum level when the shock-absorber (10) is correctly assembled, 1. e. when the outer cylindrical tube (12) is correctly closed at its top end, whereas said difference (Amp) is lower than said minimum level when the shock-absorber (10) is not cor- rectly assembled, i.e. when the outer cylindrical tube (12) is not correctly closed at its top end. 2. Method according to claim 1, wherein said steps a) and b) are carried out by measuring the Barkhausen noise only in a given point (Pi) on the outer cylindrical tube (12). 3. Method according to claim 1, wherein said steps a) and b) are carried out by meas- 

 uring the Barkhausen noise along a given measure path (p) on the outer cylindrical tube (12) and wherein said step c) is carried out by calculating the difference (Amp) between the maximum value of the Barkhausen noise measured at step b) and the maximum value of the Barkhausen noise measured at step a).
